Tests for handling exceptions in RegExp operations.

# Purpose
This code is a short script that tests the behavior of the JavaScript `RegExp` object and its methods under certain conditions. It attempts to create a new `RegExp` instance and call a non-existent method `$()` on it, which is expected to throw an error. It also tests the `compile` method of `RegExp.prototype` by calling it and passing it as an argument to a new instance of a function represented by `this.$`. Each operation is wrapped in a `try-catch` block to handle any exceptions that occur, ensuring that the script does not terminate unexpectedly. The script is likely used to verify the robustness of error handling in the `RegExp` implementation.
